comments: Taper's notes: "Time for another blast from the past? From out of my tape archive here's another complete, previously unshared, speed corrected, Friday Rock Show. It's back to Friday July 3rd 1992 and a special 'live' edition of the show featuring those wacky U.S. rockers Love/Hate who that very same night were treading the boards at the London Astoria as part of BBC Radio 1's American Music Festival. In addition to the whole of Love/Hate's live set we get choice cuts from Maiden, Dream Theater, Mama's Boys, Full House, Dio and many more."

track_listing.track: Tommy plugs the upcoming Monsters Of Rock Festival on 22nd Aug and tells us that BBC Radio 1 will be broadcasting the whole day live...He then enthuses about the 'stunning' 5 track cassette he has been listening to all day – an advance release from the mighty Thunder who will be playing at the Monsters Of Rock. Tommy says he'll play it for us just as soon as he gets permission and then quips that he might even play it next week without permission!!
Johnnie Walker promotes the American Blues show coming up tomorrow that is part of the BBC Radio 1 American Music Festival
Tommy tells us that Radio 1 recorded Badlands and Mother's Finest live on stage last night and that these shows will be broadcast on the show in the weeks ahead
BBC Radio 10.30 News @ pm - read by Brian Deacon
TV counts down the top 30 Rock/Metal singles in the UK
Outro and handover to John Peel
